source_lock.drift_handling: any mismatch on repo/path/commit/schema_version/any hash fails closed (refuses refresh, non-zero exit); a schema_version or row-count/coverage_summary change requires re-review of this design, not just a hash check

rejected.A_byte_identical_mirror_only: insufficient alone -- no Forecast-owned slot for identity-resolution status, phase-eligibility tags, or refresh provenance
rejected.C_normalized_forecast_adapter: premature at mirror stage -- normalization implies feature-design decisions reserved for a later, separate experiment-design issue; a model-ready feature table is explicitly out of scope

mirror_form.payload_transformation: none; byte-identical copy of JSON, CSV, and manifest
mirror_form.permitted_differences_from_source: none in mirrored payload bytes; wrapper is a wholly additive sibling file
mirror_form.parity_validation: sha256 of each mirrored file must equal source_lock.artifact_hashes on every refresh
mirror_form.no_phase_filtered_or_derived_projection_: the mirror-implementation issue produces exactly the four files above and nothing else; a file that physically omits a source field is a transformation, not a byte-identical mirror, and is out of scope for this stage -- any future phase-specific view is separate, later work (see temporal_eligibility.representation)

row_grain.player_id_note: TIBER-Rookies player_id is a position-prefixed name slug, locally scoped; no verified crosswalk to any Forecast/canonical (gsis_id-equivalent) player identity is known to exist
row_grain.corroboration_role: may only corroborate an already-asserted identity, never serve as the join key or a fallback for a failed player_id join

join_failure_handling.missing_forecast_player_id: row retained in mirror for audit only, marked identity_status=unresolved_to_forecast_population, excluded from any feature-bearing join
join_failure_handling.duplicate_ids: independently re-verified on every refresh (not trusted from upstream); fail closed if found
join_failure_handling.season_mismatch: row rejected for that rehearsal; no cross-season carry-forward defined for this artifact
join_failure_handling.source_rows_absent_from_forecast_populat: expected and reported (counted), not silently dropped
join_failure_handling.forecast_players_absent_from_source: expected and reported (counted), not silently dropped

field_classification.assumption: all classifications assume identity resolution (row_grain) already succeeded for that row; an unresolved-identity row is audit-only regardless of field

families.future_eligibility: conceptually pre-draft in origin but no governed as-of proof exists that a specific value was live at any cutoff -- big-board proxies change throughout the pre-draft cycle; NOT classified pre-draft-safe
no governed as-of availability proof recorded; NOT classified pre-draft-safe
combine-derived with a real unavailable-before date (the combine itself) not recorded per row; NOT classified pre-draft-safe
depends on season/stat-refresh close date not recorded per row; NOT classified pre-draft-safe
definitionally post-draft information; no future proof could make it pre-draft-eligible; eligibility in a post_draft context is separate future work, not authorized here

temporal_eligibility.leakage_risk_named: this promoted snapshot was itself generated (2026-07-10T12:00:00+00:00) after the 2026 draft concluded, commingling pre-draft-origin and post-draft-origin facts in one file; artifact generated_at must never be used as a proxy for per-field knowability

timestamp_non_interchangeability.provenance_last_verified_at: genuine event-adjacent verification date only for official_postdraft_outcome drafted rows (carries source ingested_at); a generation-date fallback or null for every other case -- must not be read as true verification for those
timestamp_non_interchangeability.future_mirror_refreshed_at: Forecast-side operational bookkeeping only, never usable to infer real-world fact knowability

dae_quan_wright_null_last_verified_at.conservative_assumption: exact knowable-as-of date is unknown; any date-sensitive post_draft rehearsal must obtain an independently-sourced signing date or exclude this row from that specific analysis

temporal_eligibility.phase_definitions_revised: pre_draft means a pinned as-of instant strictly before the 2026 draft BEGINS (Day 1 Round 1 Pick 1), not before it concludes -- a conclusion-based boundary is unsafe since it includes periods after early picks are already public. The exact draft-start instant is not pinned by this design and must come from a governed, cited source at the time a future rehearsal needs it.

availability_proof_required_before_pre_d.draft_capital: must prove the specific big_board_rank/proxy value used was the live value as of the simulated cutoff -- not proven by this artifact (no per-value as-of date recorded)
availability_proof_required_before_pre_d.age_at_entry: must prove date of birth was knowable as of the cutoff -- not proven (no governed as-of date recorded)
availability_proof_required_before_pre_d.athletic_testing: must prove the combine/testing event had occurred by the cutoff -- not proven (no combine date recorded per row)
availability_proof_required_before_pre_d.college_production: must prove the season/stat-refresh window had closed by the cutoff -- not proven (no season-close date recorded per row)
availability_proof_required_before_pre_d.official_postdraft_outcome: no proof could ever apply in a pre_draft context -- definitionally post-draft information

pre_draft.definition: as-of instant strictly before the 2026 NFL draft BEGINS; draft-start timestamp not pinned here, must be governed/cited by a future issue
pre_draft.draft_capital: temporal eligibility unresolved; NOT structurally eligible until availability proof (see above) is established
pre_draft.age_athletic_production: temporal eligibility unresolved; NOT structurally eligible until availability proof is established
pre_draft.official_postdraft_outcome: must be excluded entirely from any feature-bearing pre_draft use -- but note the mirror itself is NOT phase-filtered (see mirror_form.no_phase_filtered_or_derived_projection_created); exclusion is enforced by a future phase-specific view this design does not construct

post_draft.definition: as-of time at or after the pinned draft-start boundary having fully elapsed through the relevant pick/signing (and, for te-daequan-wright specifically, no earlier than an independently-sourced UDFA-signing date)
post_draft.draft_capital: eligible, subject to the audit-only ceiling in field_classification
post_draft.age_athletic_production: eligible, subject to the same ceiling
post_draft.official_postdraft_outcome: eligible, subject to the same ceiling

temporal_eligibility.representation: documented in this design only (field_classification, this section) as a classification future stages must respect -- NOT built into the mirror itself; the mirror-implementation stage creates no phase-specific view (see mirror_form). Constructing any such view, and establishing the availability-proof table above, is explicitly future, separate controlled-experiment/adapter-design work.
temporal_eligibility.fails_closed_because: ambiguous or unavailable timestamps, or an unestablished availability proof, default to exclusion, not inclusion-with-caveat; an excluded row/field/family can be added back once resolved, a leaked one cannot be un-leaked from a training run

observed_vs_inferred.draft_capital: inferred market-derived proxy; never overwritten by or merged with official_postdraft_outcome
prohibited: overwriting the proxy with the official outcome
collapsing both into one polymorphic draft-capital feature
converting provenance notes into numeric features
deriving a composite evidence score during mirroring

missingness.default: unavailable remains unavailable at the mirror stage
missingness.note: a model, once wired in a later separate issue, may still numerically default a null feature to 0 for arithmetic reasons at training time (matching existing player-history precedent) -- that is a future, explicitly-reviewed decision, not something this mirror design authorizes

required: full per-field {value, provenance} structure for every family
source_type, source_name, source_url, confidence, confidence_band, last_verified_at (including null), notes

validation_contract_for_future_mirror: source lock and hash validation, fail closed on any mismatch
deterministic reproduction (byte-identical mirror + wrapper apart from mirror_refreshed_at)
47 drafted + 1 udfa_signed outcome parity, re-verified against mirrored JSON
JSON/CSV agreement (identical player_id population and order)
no duplicate or fuzzy identity resolution
observed/inferred provenance preservation with no cross-contamination
temporal-eligibility assertions (te-daequan-wright last_verified_at stays null; no phase-filtered/derived projection is created by this stage)
source drift fails closed (simulated hash mismatch test)
no model or production imports reference the mirror (repo-wide import check)
no availability claim asserted (wrapper does not label any field pre_draft_safe/experiment_eligible or equivalent)

lifecycle_placement.reference: docs/capabilities/README.md governed capability path (eight stages; none compressed or skipped)
lifecycle_placement.current_stage: between owned_source_artifact (done) and mirror_rehearsal_path (this design governs the next issue)
lifecycle_placement.validation_disjoint_origin_requirement: at least TWO disjoint populations/seasons, evaluated independently with no averaging across origins, per docs/capabilities/README.md's validation-stage requirement -- a single disjoint origin is not sufficient
future_issues_required: controlled experiment / rehearsal design (first establishes the per-family availability-proof table and pins the governed draft-start boundary from a cited source, before proposing any feature extraction)
experiment execution across at least two disjoint populations/seasons
threshold review (decides only whether a binding-review issue may be opened)
binding review (production-path inventory + real leakage audit; decides only whether an implementation issue may be opened)
implementation (narrowest safe scope, inert by default behind explicit opt-in)
activation verification (independent re-exercise from the default branch)
production binding / non-default activation, only with explicit human sign-off, only after activation verification passes

decision_scope: authorizes only a separate, bounded mirror-implementation issue conforming to the selected mirror form and validation contract; does not authorize experimentation, feature activation, predictive use, downstream consumption, production binding, or UI activation
non_goals: no fixture written under data/fixtures/tiberRookies/ (path proposed, not created)
no phase-filtered or otherwise derived projection defined or authorized (the only artifact form authorized is the full byte-identical mirror)
no claim that any field improves MAE, RMSE, calibration, or fantasy-point prediction
no claim that any field is available or safe to use at any specific pre-draft as-of cutoff (availability-proof table not satisfied for any of the four candidate families)
no TIBER-Rookies change of any kind
no canonical player-ID crosswalk built (gap named, not closed)
no draft-start boundary timestamp pinned (requirement named, not satisfied)
